Manage opt-in anonymous usage telemetry

Usage:
  laurel telemetry [--endpoint <url>] <subcommand>

Arguments:
  subcommand          `enable`, `disable`, or `status`

End of options:
  Use `--` before positional values that start with `-`, for example `laurel new post -- --config`.

Options:
  --endpoint <url>    Set the stored telemetry endpoint when enabling. LAUREL_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT overrides it per run
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --endpoint → LAUREL_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel telemetry status
  laurel telemetry enable
  laurel telemetry enable --endpoint https://telemetry.example.test/v1/usage
  LAUREL_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT=http://127.0.0.1:8787/usage laurel build
  laurel telemetry disable
